subject: Biology
title: Grasslands and Savannas
description: A quick, practical guide to Grasslands and Savannas.

hook.text: Grasslands cover a quarter of Earth's land surface. They're too dry for forests, too wet for deserts.

buildup.text: African savannas host the greatest wildlife spectacles—millions of wildebeest migrate each year.

discovery.text: Grass survives grazing because it grows from the base, not the tip. Mow it and it bounces back.

twist.text: Much of the world's grassland has been converted to farmland. It's the most transformed biome.

climax.text: Grassland soils store enormous amounts of carbon in deep root systems—more stable than forest carbon.

punchline.text: Grass looks simple. It's anything but.

quiz.question: How does grass survive being grazed or mowed?
options: It grows from the base, not the tip
It regrows from seeds each time
